title: I Buried Sid

jokes.text: Yeah, but what, like a penny every 11 years? I mean, what is it... what is it now, 32 cents. Whoo-ooh-ooh... is it 32 cents? That sounds high.
jokes.setup: Larry defending post office against complaints about rising stamp prices
jokes.punchline: Realizes mid-bit he doesn't actually know current stamp price, undermining his own argument
jokes.explanation: Classic stand-up misdirection - comedian's confidence crumbles when he realizes he doesn't know what he's talking about. Well-executed self-sabotage.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Yeah, but what, like a penny every 11 years? I mean, what is it... what is it now, 32 cents. Whoo-ooh-ooh... is it 32 cents? That sounds high.

jokes.text: You're a funny guy.
jokes.setup: Response to Larry's stand-up routine
jokes.punchline: Understated, potentially sarcastic response to his performance
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that could be sincere or cutting - the ambiguity creates the humor in typical Larry Sanders style.

dialogue.line: You're a funny guy.

jokes.text: when celebrity relationships go public, and... I don't know. It starts to ruin everything.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing keeping relationship secret
jokes.punchline: Pretends it's about protecting the relationship when really he's embarrassed/uncommitted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders character moment - self-serving rationalization disguised as wisdom about celebrity culture.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: when celebrity relationships go public, and... I don't know. It starts to ruin everything.

jokes.text: Sounds captivating.
jokes.setup: Larry says he was home alone last night
jokes.punchline: Dry, sarcastic response highlighting how boring his evening sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of words for maximum sarcastic impact. One word that cuts deep.

dialogue.line: Sounds captivating.

jokes.text: Screw you, Larry! My mother and I always hated you!
jokes.setup: Sid performing Liza Minnelli impression during rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Breaks character to deliver personal insult as if he's actually Liza
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - bad impression becomes genuine personal attack, but delivered as if still in character. Shows Sid's delusion.

dialogue.speaker: Sid as Liza Minnelli
dialogue.line: Screw you, Larry! My mother and I always hated you!

jokes.text: Tour de force. New face in town, Sid. Hilarious.
jokes.setup: Sid's terrible Liza Minnelli impression
jokes.punchline: Larry's obviously sarcastic praise, delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry passive-aggression - builds Sid up with showbiz clichés while clearly meaning the opposite.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Tour de force. New face in town, Sid. Hilarious.

jokes.text: It means that you're a fucking freak.
jokes.setup: Sid asks what Hank meant by calling his performance a freak show
jokes.punchline: Hank's blunt, brutal clarification
jokes.explanation: Pure Hank Kingsley - no filter, maximum cruelty delivered with complete sincerity. Economical brutality.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: It means that you're a fucking freak.

jokes.text: You don't know the fucking difference between being laughed with and being laughed at.
jokes.setup: Hank continuing his brutal assessment of Sid
jokes.punchline: Devastating insight that's both cruel and accurate
jokes.explanation: Brutally incisive line that cuts to the heart of entertainment industry delusion. Becomes tragically prophetic given Sid's suicide.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: You don't know the fucking difference between being laughed with and being laughed at.

jokes.text: He said, 'is there a man in your life with a 'd' and an 'a' and a 'd?' I said, 'yeah, that's my dad.'
jokes.setup: Artie explaining how psychics work with Kreskin example
jokes.punchline: Reveals the obvious answer to the psychic's fishing question
jokes.explanation: Good structure showing how psychics use obvious guesses. Artie's deadpan delivery sells the 'revelation.'

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: He said, 'is there a man in your life with a 'd' and an 'a' and a 'd?' I said, 'yeah, that's my dad.'

jokes.text: On most nights, you'd be batting 1,000.
jokes.setup: Psychic says she sees a new woman in Artie's life
jokes.punchline: Artie's self-deprecating admission about his usual lack of female companionship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie moment - gruff self-awareness about his romantic life delivered with typical cynicism.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: On most nights, you'd be batting 1,000.

jokes.text: Is there also an 'h,' and an 'r,' char?
jokes.setup: Psychic fishing for woman's name with 'c' and 'a'
jokes.punchline: Artie sarcastically spells out 'char' revealing the psychic's obvious technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deflation of psychic pretense. Artie cuts through the mysticism with perfect timing and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Is there also an 'h,' and an 'r,' char?

jokes.text: You have the worse handwriting than stephen hawking.
jokes.setup: Phil frustrated with someone's handwriting
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Stephen Hawking, who has ALS and can't write by hand
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's a bit cheap - relies on disability for shock value rather than clever insight.

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: You have the worse handwriting than stephen hawking.

jokes.text: Good for you. You gotta set an example. No. I mean, he killed himself.
jokes.setup: Hank thinks Artie is saying Sid should be fired for being late
jokes.punchline: Artie reveals Sid is actually dead by suicide
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark misdirection - Hank's enthusiasm for punishment collides with tragic reality. Expertly structured reveal.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Good for you. You gotta set an example. No. I mean, he killed himself.

jokes.text: Some nights you have such special... special show where you have terrific guests, and some nights, honestly, you just have, you know, average guests. But tonight's one of those nights where we have... have really good guests.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to do his monologue after learning about Sid's death
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to focus or deliver coherent thoughts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy - Larry's professional facade crumbling under emotional pressure. Pure character-driven humor.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Some nights you have such special... special show where you have terrific guests, and some nights, honestly, you just have, you know, average guests. But tonight's one of those nights where we have... have really good guests.

jokes.text: I wonder if they'll ask me to be a pallbearer? Because, you know, I have a bad... back? Shoulder?
jokes.setup: Larry considering funeral arrangements
jokes.punchline: Already planning excuse to avoid physical labor at funeral
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Larry selfishness - turning a man's death into his own inconvenience. The hesitation between 'back' and 'shoulder' adds authenticity.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I wonder if they'll ask me to be a pallbearer? Because, you know, I have a bad... back? Shoulder?

jokes.text: I told him he was a... a... a fourth rate Larry bud melman.
jokes.setup: Hank confessing what he said to Sid before his suicide
jokes.punchline: Specific, devastating comparison to another TV show oddball
jokes.explanation: Brutally specific insult that shows Hank's cruelty while referencing Larry Bud Melman adds industry insider humor.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I told him he was a... a... a fourth rate Larry bud melman.

jokes.text: Call rosie grier. Ask him if he'll come over and pray with me.
jokes.setup: Hank feeling guilty about Sid's death
jokes.punchline: His solution is to call specific celebrity for prayer session
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd celebrity name-drop for spiritual help. Perfectly captures Hank's Hollywood-centric worldview even in crisis.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Call rosie grier. Ask him if he'll come over and pray with me.

jokes.text: cue card men never die. They just get whited out.
jokes.setup: Larry giving eulogy for Sid at funeral
jokes.punchline: Pun about cue card correction fluid in context of death
jokes.explanation: Decent pun but inappropriate for funeral setting. Shows Larry's tone-deafness but the wordplay itself is somewhat forced.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: cue card men never die. They just get whited out.

jokes.text: No flipping. Shut up, Phil.
jokes.setup: Phil making cue card reference after Larry's bad joke
jokes.punchline: Hank telling him to shut up
jokes.explanation: Good callback to show business terminology, Hank's timing is perfect

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: No flipping. Shut up, Phil.

jokes.text: It didn't work, but you know why? Because you went so long. You know, you killed the whole audience.
jokes.setup: Larry's failed funeral joke
jokes.punchline: Hank says Larry 'killed' the audience at a funeral
jokes.explanation: Dark irony in 'killed the audience' at funeral. Hank's critical assessment wrapped in unintentionally morbid language.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: It didn't work, but you know why? Because you went so long. You know, you killed the whole audience.

jokes.text: A very gifted actor who people laughed with and not at.
jokes.setup: Larry talking to Sid's brother
jokes.punchline: Directly contradicts Hank's earlier brutal assessment of Sid
jokes.explanation: Clever callback to Hank's devastating line, now ironically reversed for the grieving brother. Shows Larry's shameless revisionism.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: A very gifted actor who people laughed with and not at.

jokes.text: Um, you know, snaps.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to downplay his treatment of Sid to his brother
jokes.punchline: Calling verbal abuse 'snaps' like it's playful banter
jokes.explanation: Perfect euphemism that shows Larry's inability to acknowledge his cruelty. 'Snaps' minimizes genuine harm.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Um, you know, snaps.

jokes.text: Got a lot of unpaid bills, and, uh, insurance policy, borrowed money, and I'd like to help, but I'm living on unemployment...
jokes.setup: Sid's brother explaining financial situation
jokes.punchline: Transparent attempt to get money from Larry
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable gold-digging at funeral, fits the show's cynical tone

dialogue.speaker: Fred
dialogue.line: Got a lot of unpaid bills, and, uh, insurance policy, borrowed money, and I'd like to help, but I'm living on unemployment...

jokes.text: because I loved him.
jokes.setup: Sid's brother asks why Larry will pay for funeral and bills
jokes.punchline: Larry's obviously false emotional claim
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry - guilt-driven generosity disguised as love. The pause before delivery suggests even he doesn't believe it.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: because I loved him.

jokes.text: Fake things. I mean, you know... oh. Fake dinosaurs.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Laura Dern about running from dinosaurs in movies
jokes.punchline: His delayed realization that movie dinosaurs aren't real
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's tendency to ask obvious questions and then have delayed comprehension. Mild but character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Fake things. I mean, you know... oh. Fake dinosaurs.

jokes.text: Wasn't it at a function where I don't remember you?
jokes.setup: Larry and Laura Dern discussing if they've met before
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally honest admission of forgetting her
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social cringe - Larry's honesty about forgetting a famous actress. The phrasing is perfectly awkward.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Wasn't it at a function where I don't remember you?

jokes.text: Fuck me.
jokes.setup: Larry sees Sid's $2,400 Discover card bill
jokes.punchline: His immediate reaction to the debt he's inheriting
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction that shows Larry's regret about offering to pay bills. Economy works but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Fuck me.

jokes.text: Uh, does anybody take discover anymore? I mean, was this guy current on anything?
jokes.setup: Larry looking at Sid's bills
jokes.punchline: Criticizing dead man's choice of outdated credit card
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about outdated payment methods, made darker by criticizing the recently deceased.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Uh, does anybody take discover anymore? I mean, was this guy current on anything?

jokes.text: What did you say to Sid? When? Right after rehearsal when you walked away from me and made a beeline backstage, probably to tell him how rotten he was and how much better you'd be in the sketch, right?
jokes.setup: Hank confronting Larry about Sid's death
jokes.punchline: Hank's specific, accurate accusation about Larry's motives
jokes.explanation: Hank's detective work revealing Larry's predictable selfishness. Well-structured accusation that hits multiple character notes.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What did you say to Sid? When? Right after rehearsal when you walked away from me and made a beeline backstage, probably to tell him how rotten he was and how much better you'd be in the sketch, right?

jokes.text: We... we never got to that.
jokes.setup: Hank asks what kind of car Sid had after Larry claims they talked about cars
jokes.punchline: Larry's weak lie immediately exposed
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Larry being caught in a lie. His hesitation and weak excuse are beautifully transparent.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: We... we never got to that.

jokes.text: Really? What kind of car did Sid have? We... we never got to that.
jokes.setup: Phil claims they talked about cars
jokes.punchline: Admits they never actually discussed car types
jokes.explanation: Good exposure of Phil's lie - he can't even maintain his fake story

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Really? What kind of car did Sid have?

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: We... we never got to that.

jokes.text: He had her stashed in a little place in the valley. He used to bang her at lunch.
jokes.setup: Hank explaining Sid's secret girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Crude but specific details about their affair
jokes.explanation: Hank's blunt delivery of gossip. The specificity of 'at lunch' adds absurd detail to the revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: He had her stashed in a little place in the valley. He used to bang her at lunch.

jokes.text: I thought his wife was dead. Sid. No. I guess he liked to tell people that.
jokes.setup: Larry's confusion about Sid's marital status
jokes.punchline: Revealing Sid lied about his wife being dead
jokes.explanation: Dark and brilliant - shows how far Sid would go to avoid dealing with his personal problems

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I thought his wife was dead. Sid. No. I guess he liked to tell people that.

jokes.text: I guess he liked to tell people that.
jokes.setup: Larry thought Sid's wife was dead
jokes.punchline: Reveals Sid lied about his wife being dead
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation that Sid was pathological liar even about basic facts. Adds layer to his character posthumously.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I guess he liked to tell people that.

jokes.text: Can you imagine killing yourself over a couple of chicks?
jokes.setup: Learning about Sid's romantic troubles
jokes.punchline: Hank's callous dismissal of suicide motivations
jokes.explanation: Pure Hank insensitivity - reduces suicide to simple relationship problems. Dark but character-consistent.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Can you imagine killing yourself over a couple of chicks?

jokes.text: He... he wants you to do the sketch.
jokes.setup: Psychic claiming to contact dead Sid
jokes.punchline: Dead Sid still concerned about the Liza Minnelli sketch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist callback - dead performer still obsessing over his terrible sketch. Perfect blend of supernatural and showbiz satire.

dialogue.speaker: Char the psychic
dialogue.line: He... he wants you to do the sketch.

jokes.text: We'll have you on another night. No, you won't.
jokes.setup: Larry's polite dismissal of Laura Dern
jokes.punchline: Her immediate, blunt correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Laura Dern cuts through Larry's showbiz politeness with brutal honesty.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: We'll have you on another night.

dialogue.speaker: Laura Dern
dialogue.line: No, you won't.

jokes.text: I would love to cook for you, and... and, um... why don't we take a break?
jokes.setup: Larry getting too personal on air with Laura Dern
jokes.punchline: Has to cut to break to escape the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry on-air meltdown moment, good cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I would love to cook for you, and... and, um... why don't we take a break?

jokes.text: Give my best to your mom and your dad, dern old bruce.
jokes.setup: Larry saying goodbye to Laura Dern
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on her name and father Bruce Dern
jokes.explanation: Terrible pun that perfectly captures Larry's awkward attempts at charm

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Give my best to your mom and your dad, dern old bruce.

jokes.text: It was like when you were interviewing Dan rather.
jokes.setup: Hank complimenting Larry's interview with Laura Dern
jokes.punchline: Compares it to interviewing a serious news anchor
jokes.explanation: Hank's backhanded compliment comparing flirtation to professional journalism. Subtle dig at Larry's interview style.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: It was like when you were interviewing Dan rather.

jokes.text: Aw, screw you, Larry. I gotta tell you something. My mom and I always hated you.
jokes.setup: Hank performing the Liza Minnelli sketch that Sid originated
jokes.punchline: Delivers the exact same insult Sid used
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Sid's version - shows how the sketch format survives the performer. Poignant and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Aw, screw you, Larry. I gotta tell you something. My mom and I always hated you.

jokes.text: Well, at you, but hilarious. You were good.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if audience was laughing with him or at him
jokes.punchline: Hank confirms they were laughing AT him but calls it good
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to Hank's earlier brutal insight about Sid. Now applied to Larry with twisted encouragement.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Well, at you, but hilarious. You were good.

jokes.text: Were they, uh... were they laughing with me or were they laughing at me? Well, at you, but hilarious.
jokes.setup: Phil asks the crucial question about audience reaction
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally honest answer echoes Phil's earlier cruelty to Sid
jokes.explanation: Masterful callback that completes the circle - Larry now delivers the same cruel truth Phil gave Sid

dialogue.speaker: Phil
dialogue.line: Were they, uh... were they laughing with me or were they laughing at me?

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Well, at you, but hilarious.

jokes.text: Oh, Sid, you fuck. You tricked me, buddy.
jokes.setup: Hank successfully performing Sid's sketch
jokes.punchline: Realizes Sid manipulated him from beyond the grave
jokes.explanation: Perfect moment of realization - Hank understands Sid got his revenge by making him perform the sketch. Dark but affectionate.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Oh, Sid, you fuck. You tricked me, buddy.

jokes.text: I just ran into a psychic in the parking lot who told me that you and Laura are an item. Is this the truth? Is this the truth?
jokes.setup: Jerry Stiller as guest on the show
jokes.punchline: Immediately exposes Larry's secret relationship on live TV
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the psychic subplot, Larry's relationship secrecy, and creates maximum cringe by exposing it on air.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry Stiller
dialogue.line: I just ran into a psychic in the parking lot who told me that you and Laura are an item. Is this the truth? Is this the truth?

tmdb_overview: Everyone takes Sid's death hard, especially Hank who thinks he may have pushed Sid over the edge.
